name._value: Silas Irons in Need of an Apprentice

0._value: The master blacksmith of #m10004000#, #p504#, seems to be looking for someone worthy of becoming his apprentice.

1._value: The master blacksmith of #m10004000#, #p504#, has given me a test to see if I’m fit to become his apprentice. He says a blacksmith needs proper strength and knowledge, and told me to defeat monsters around #m10004000# and bring back raw ore. It looks like raw ore can be obtained from monsters.

2._value: I passed the test given by #p504#, the master blacksmith of #m10004000#. Does this mean I can become a true blacksmith in Maple World?

0._value: There have been more monsters around town lately, so I’ve been busier than ever. It sure would be nice to have an apprentice to help me out… Oh! You there! Ever thought about becoming a #bBlacksmith#k?

1._value: Huh? What’s a blacksmith? Hahaha! You’re honest, I’ll give you that. A blacksmith is #bsomeone who works with metal ore to craft various metal armor pieces#k. You still look like a rookie, but something tells me you might make a decent apprentice. What do you say?

0._value: Good choice! But even I can’t just take anyone as my apprentice. Let’s see… Right! I’ll need to give you a simple test to see if you’ve got potential.

1._value: To become a blacksmith, you’ll need not only technique, but also strength and knowledge—understanding materials, knowing how to obtain them, and having the ability to gather them. Go defeat #r#o6#s and #o11#s#k near #m10004000# and bring me #b5 #t4010000#s. Then we can talk further.

0._value: Looks like you’re busy right now? That’s fine. In Maple World, getting stronger isn’t only about defeating monsters. Learning how to work with metal is another path to reaching new heights. Come back anytime if you change your mind.
It seems you defeated the monsters I mentioned and collected the ore properly! With this, I suppose I can teach you my craft.
From this moment on, I recognize you as a blacksmith. I’ve added several blacksmithing skills to your #bCrafting Journal#k—go take a look.

1._value: And actually, I had you gather those ores for your own sake. You should try crafting a few items with them. Just remember: if you want to forge items, you’ll need to use the #b#p800010##k next to me.

2._value: As you craft more items, you’ll gain experience and level up, allowing you to make even more kinds of equipment. But keep in mind—raising your maximum blacksmithing skill level also requires raising your character level, so don’t neglect either side of your growth.

3._value: That’s all I can teach you for now. You’re still a beginner, but don’t worry. With steady effort, you’ll be able to reach the level of a true master someday—just like me. Come find me anytime if you have questions!

0._value: It looks like you haven’t defeated all the monsters I mentioned. If you can’t even handle that, you’re nowhere near ready to become a blacksmith.
It looks like you haven’t collected all the materials I asked for. If you can’t even do that, you’re nowhere near ready to become a blacksmith.
